user.title: DOOM
user.description: Hell's forces have breached your smart home. Set up DOOM to fight back with a playable card right on your dashboard. Rip and tear, until it is done.

abort.already_configured: DOOM is already ripping and tearing on your system. There can only be one Doom Slayer.

state.rip_and_tear: Rip and tear, until it is done.
state.huge_guts: You're huge! That means you have huge guts! Rip and tear!
state.rage_brutal: They are rage, brutal, without mercy. But you. You will be worse.
state.first_age: In the first age, in the first battle, when the shadows first lengthened, one stood...
state.against_all_evil: Against all the evil that Hell can conjure, all the wickedness that mankind can produce, we will send unto them only you.
state.only_thing_they_fear: The only thing they fear... is you.
state.secret_area: Look at that! You found a secret area.
state.be_careful: Warning: Who's there? Be careful, that could be a trap.
state.infested_starport: You've pushed deep into the infested starport.
state.created_1993: DOOM was created by id Software and released on December 10, 1993.
state.carmack_engine: John Carmack programmed the DOOM engine in approximately 5 months.
state.team_of_six: The original DOOM was developed by a team of just 6 people.
state.deathmatch: DOOM popularized the term 'deathmatch' for multiplayer gaming.
state.bfg: The BFG stands for 'Big Fragging Gun' (officially).
state.shareware_bbs: DOOM was originally distributed as shareware through bulletin board systems.
state.cacodemon_design: The Cacodemon's design was inspired by a creature from the cover of the D&D Manual of the Planes.
state.moons_of_mars: The original DOOM is set on the moons of Mars: Phobos and Deimos.
state.bobby_prince: Bobby Prince composed the DOOM soundtrack, famously inspired by heavy metal bands like Metallica, Pantera, and Slayer.
state.open_source: The DOOM source code was released as open source on December 23, 1997.
state.ported_everywhere: DOOM has been ported to nearly every computing platform imaginable, including ATMs, pregnancy tests, and smart fridges.
state.doomguy: The protagonist of DOOM is commonly known as 'Doomguy' or the 'Doom Slayer'.
state.no_look_up_down: The original DOOM had no ability to look up or down.
state.wad_modding: DOOM was one of the first games to support modding through WAD files.
state.iddqd: The IDDQD cheat code enables God mode in the original DOOM.
state.idkfa: IDKFA gives you all keys, full ammo, and all weapons in DOOM.
state.spider_mastermind: The Spider Mastermind was the final boss of the original DOOM.
state.knee_deep_downloads: DOOM's shareware episode 'Knee Deep in the Dead' was downloaded millions of times in its first year.
state.super_shotgun: DOOM II introduced the Super Shotgun, one of the most beloved weapons in FPS history.
state.cyberdemon: The Cyberdemon is one of the most iconic enemies in gaming history.
state.more_than_windows: At its peak, DOOM was estimated to be installed on more computers than Windows 95.
state.imp_fireball: The Imp's fireball was one of the first projectile attacks in a 3D first-person shooter.
state.bsp_rendering: The DOOM engine renders pseudo-3D graphics using a technique called binary space partitioning (BSP).
state.pinky_demon: The Pinky demon was named after its original bright pink color in early development.
state.speedrun: Speedrunners can complete DOOM's first episode in under 5 minutes.
state.romero_head: John Romero's head on a stick is the real final boss of DOOM II.
state.floppy_disks: The original DOOM floppy disk installer required four 3.5-inch disks.
state.e1m1: E1M1 ('Hangar') is one of the most recognized video game levels ever created.
state.popular_1995: DOOM was so popular in 1995 that it was estimated to be on more PCs than Microsoft's Windows 95.
state.chainsaw_evil_dead: The chainsaw in DOOM was inspired by the Evil Dead movies.
state.lost_soul: The Lost Soul enemy was originally intended to be a thrown skull from the Pain Elemental, which was added in DOOM II.
state.blessed_server: A Catholic priest once blessed a LAN server before a DOOM deathmatch tournament.
state.resolution_320x200: The original DOOM runs at a resolution of 320x200 pixels.
state.faster_processors: DOOM forced Intel and other companies to accelerate the development of faster processors.
state.mars_setting: The Mars setting was chosen because John Carmack thought demons from Mars sounded cool.
